meaning: n.选举之投票,民意测验v.投票,获得选票,选举中获得
examples: The final result of the poll will be known tomorrow.
A MORI poll showed that 68% of people opposed the ban.
A recent poll gave Campbell a 68 percent support rating.
I'm going to be losing poll workers and they're very hard to find, to begin with.
This prompted a New Economy consultant named Geoffrey Moore to send out ane-mail poll.
